title: Citric acid nuclei isolation from frozen oesophageal tissue

RNALater-ICE pre-treatment of frozen tissue (Important: perform at least one day before nuclei isolation):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":0,"length":109},{"style":"bold","offset":48,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7u4jp","text":"(A) Pre-chill a minimum of 10 volumes (compared to the sample mass/ volume) of RNAlater-ICE at 70 or -80°C in a polypropylene tube.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ah7oc","text":"(B) Place frozen tissue (from LN2 or -80°C), no larger than 0.5 cm in the smallest dimension, into the RNAlater-ICE. It is important that the RNAlater-ICE stays cold and the tissue remains frozen, so work quickly. Set up the tubes of RNAlater-ICE and tissue samples in a container of dry ice. For the frozen tissue, simply pipet at least 10 volumes of pre-chilled RNAlater-ICE into the tube. Tightly cap the tube and invert several times to mix.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bu2i6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7dem9","text":"Soak the tissue in RNAlater-ICE at -20°C for at least 16 hours. steps: Weigh tissue. 30-40mg of tissue should be used per experiment. If not using the entire sample, quickly remove the tissue required by chopping sample in a petri dish chilled on dry ice (For RNA Later Ice samples see 1.1) with a cold scalpel. Return unused tissue to LN 2 .
If using tissue stored in RNA Later Ice, carry out steps 1 and 2 on wet ice. Pre-chill a petri dish on dry ice and chop tissue into very small pieces (<0.3cm) using a cold scalpel.
Add tissue to pre-chilled dounce homogenizer containing 2ml nuclei lysis buffer.
While keeping the dounce on ice, homogenize for 5 strokes using pestle A.

Homogenize with 5 strokes of pestle A followed by 20 strokes with pestle B. The tissue should be well homogenised and the solution in the dounce should be cloudy with no large tissue peices remaining.
Filter the contents of the dounce through a 30μm cell strainer. Use an additional 1ml of nuclei lysis buffer to rinse the dounce. Filter the 1ml lysis buffer through the strainer.
Centrifuge the nuclei suspension at 500 x g at 4°C for 5 minutes.
Carefully remove and discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 1ml of nuclei lysis buffer.
Centrifuge the nuclei suspension at 500 x g, 4°C for 5 minutes. Carefully remove the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 200μL nuclei resuspension buffer.
Remove 10μL of nuclei suspension and stain 1:1 with Dapi. If a high nuclei yield is anticipated, dilute the 10μL of nuclei suspension 1:5 with PBS before staining.
Incubate tissue on ice in the dounce for 5 minutes.
creator: Lucy Kimbley
